title: MPC of a Mobile Manipulator
summary: Model predictive control driving a mobile manipulator to a target end-effector pose with real-time obstacle avoidance.
problem: Drive a mobile manipulator to a target end-effector configuration while respecting actuation limits and avoiding a circular obstacle — a non-convex, constrained trajectory problem.
solution: At each timestep, a finite-horizon optimization problem is solved over the system's kinematic and dynamic model. The obstacle is encoded as an inequality constraint, keeping the planned trajectory feasible and collision-free throughout the horizon.
result: Controller successfully reaches goal configurations while satisfying all constraints. Demonstrated robustness to varying obstacle positions and different initial configurations.

title: Fader Networks: Controllable Image Attribute Editing
summary: PyTorch reimplementation of Fader Networks (Lample et al., NeurIPS 2017) for continuous, identity-preserving image attribute editing.
problem: Learn a latent representation that is invariant to chosen attributes (smile, gender, glasses) so that attribute values can be slid continuously at inference without altering identity.
solution: Encoder-decoder with latent adversarial training: a discriminator tries to predict the attribute from the encoding, while the encoder learns to fool it, decorrelating attribute information from identity. At inference, attributes are injected into the decoder to produce realistic edits.
result: Reproduced the paper's qualitative results on CelebA. Attribute edits are smooth and identity-preserving across the full slider range. Ablations on adversarial weight confirmed the decorrelation mechanism.

title: Attitude Estimation and Control for a Drone
summary: Quaternion-based attitude estimation and closed-loop control for a drone, comparing a linear and a nonlinear estimator.
problem: Recover roll, pitch, and yaw reliably and close the attitude control loop — without the singularities that affect Euler-angle formulations at large angles.
solution: Implemented two estimators: a linear one that estimates Euler angles then converts to quaternion, and a nonlinear one that operates directly in quaternion space. Both feed the same control loop, closing the loop on orientation.
result: The nonlinear estimator outperforms the linear baseline at large angles where gimbal lock would otherwise cause instability. Analysis quantified accuracy, convergence speed, and computational overhead of each formulation.

title: Autonomous Vehicle Navigation with ROS2
summary: Full autonomy stack for a ground vehicle: line following, LIDAR-based obstacle avoidance, corridor navigation, and goal-scoring, all running in ROS2.
problem: Build a complete autonomous navigation stack that handles four distinct challenges in the same environment: lane following, dynamic obstacle avoidance, narrow-corridor traversal, and precision maneuvering to push a ball into a goal.
solution: Camera pipeline for line detection feeds a visual-servoing controller. A LIDAR-based reactive layer handles obstacle avoidance and corridor centering. Each challenge runs as a separate ROS2 node, composable into a unified mission stack.
result: Successfully completed all four navigation challenges in simulation and on hardware. Clean modular architecture allows each behavior to be tested and swapped independently.
